title: Elleanthus coriifolius
taxonomy: Elleanthus coriifolius is classified in the family Gesneriaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gesneriad family. This family comprises about 3,000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Elleanthus coriifolius, as part of the Gesneriaceae family, is a type of epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants, often in the forest canopy. The exact classification of Elleanthus coriifolius includes its genus, which is Elleanthus, and its species, Elleanthus coriifolius. Elleanthus coriifolius is also classified in the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that includes many other species of gesneriads and other related plants.

intro: Elleanthus coriifolius, also known as Elleanthus, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Elleanthus coriifolius include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Elleanthus coriifolius is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
